A.rule: Catalan numbers: C_n=(1/(n+1))*binomial(2n,n), starting at n=0
Successive differences are squares of consecutive primes
Odd-indexed terms are successive squares; even-indexed terms are successive factorials

B.rule: Start at 4 and successively add squares of consecutive primes starting with 3².
Odd positions are consecutive squares; even positions are consecutive factorials.

C.rule: Multiply by successive integers, alternating plus 1 and minus 1
Odd terms are consecutive squares; even terms are consecutive factorials

D.rule: Start at 4 and cumulatively add squares of consecutive odd primes

E.rule: Start at 4 and add successive squares of prime numbers
Odd positions are consecutive squares; even positions are consecutive factorials
